name: a multi-fill trace becomes the ordered who-fell-when, first at zero seconds
intent: solveSequence maps each trace entry to {cell, atSeconds} with atSeconds = (at - t0)/1000, t0 = min(at). Strictly increasing at yields the entries in order, the first at 0. INV-6: cells and times only, no userId.

name: two fills at the same instant order by ascending seq
intent: An (at, seq) tie breaks by ascending seq: cell 8 (seq 2) precedes cell 9 (seq 3) though both share at 6000. Pins the secondary sort key.

name: the sequence re-sorts on at, not on trace order, when clock skew crosses seq order
intent: THE KEY CASE. The trace arrives seq-ordered but a later-seq entry carries an earlier at (clock skew across writers). solveSequence sorts by (at, seq), so the output is who-fell-when by at, not an echo of trace order. t0 = min(at) = 5000; the seq-1 fill (at 20000) lands third, not first.

name: a single-instant solve is all zero seconds, ordered by seq
intent: Every entry shares one at (tEnd == t0), so every atSeconds is 0 and the (at, seq) sort falls entirely to seq. Mirrors the single-instant parity the other projections pin.

name: an empty trace is an empty sequence
intent: No fills means no order to project; solveSequence returns []. Mirrors the empty-trace parity momentum and moments pin.
